danger_signals.meaning: Escalation of threat level; final gauntlet danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Iori Yagami (new character), Billy Kane, Eiji Kisaragi grouped together
danger_signals.meaning: Antagonistic faction; threat to protagonist's dominance

safety_signals.visual_form: Kyo Kusanagi as player protagonist with Benimaru Nikaido and Goro Daimon
Three-member team composition with customization available
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and support system; balanced gameplay state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Iori Yagami (new character introducing rival narrative)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Antagonistic positioning; threat to Kyo's supremacy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stole American Sports Team invitations; subversive action

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Terry Bogard, Andy Bogard, Joe Higashi
Ryo Sakazaki, Robert Garcia, Takuma Sakazaki
Heidern (military leader figure), Ralf Jones, Clark Still
Rugal Bernstein (crime boss archetype); Omega Rugal (enhanced form)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Omega Rugal represents powered-up/demonic state through Orochi influence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament organizer; final boss; uses snake demon power

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Team-based bracket progression; multiple faction teams present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Teams compete sequentially until all defeated; escalating difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rugal Bernstein as tournament promoter and final boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rugal survived previous explosion; orchestrates revenge tournament; corrupts Saisyu Kusanagi

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Omega Rugal as enhanced final form; Orochi snake demon power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss character becomes stronger; represents supernatural threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Iori Yagami introduced as new character; forms Rival Team
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Iori becomes Kyo's rival; positioning for long-term narrative arc (Orochi Saga)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Saisyu Kusanagi appears as sub-boss opponent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Saisyu fights player; later revealed as brainwashed; indicates external control threat

environmental_storytelling.example: Rugal sends invitations signed 'R' to previous tournament teams; Rival Team steals American Sports Team invitations, preventing their attendance
Rugal thought dead from previous game explosion, but survived; returns to organize new tournament
Fatal Fury Team and Art of Fighting Team appear alongside original teams, indicating expanded tournament scope and multi-franchise world
Iori Yagami introduced as new character in antagonistic Rival Team; establishes long-term narrative rivalry
Saisyu Kusanagi (father) appears as playable character for first time; reveals he is brainwashed, indicating family-based threat dynamics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Organizes tournament for revenge; survives previous defeat; transforms into Omega Rugal with enhanced power
Appears as sub-boss; revealed to be under mind control; indicates external manipulation
Forms Rival Team; positions as Kyo's narrative opponent; introduces conflict to protagonist's dominance
Leads Hero Team; tournament winner from previous game; target of revenge; establishes narrative continuity
Steals invitations; blocks American Sports Team participation; presents systemic threat through sabotage

notes: KOF '95 communicates world-state primarily through narrative positioning and team composition rather than visual environment details. The game emphasizes faction identity through character grouping and role assignment (Hero, Rival, Criminal). World-state danger is conveyed through boss escalation (Omega Rugal), character corruption (Saisyu brainwashing), and narrative threat (Rugal's survival and revenge motive). Safety is signaled through established protagonist alignment and team customization agency. The introduction of the Orochi Saga beginning indicates a shift toward supernatural/demonic threats. Fighting game paradigm limits environmental storytelling; communication occurs through character selection, boss progression, and narrative reveals between matches.
